nts1.d: Classic Jamaican records from the "Rocksteady Daddy" Asher G.
Palm Wine Club was born in the basement of the London EDITION almost eight years ago. Evolving from party to radio show to travelling arts club - Palm Wine Club celebrates art, life + enjoyment.

nts1.n: NOEL SPIVA w/ Moochie - Yacht Rock Special
nts1.d:
St. Louis multidimensional visual artist Noel Spiva brings a regular hour of warming, soulful selections merging smooth rap & inspiring jazzy tracks…
From the host: "Moochie joins the show today with a very special Yacht Rock guest mix"
London producer & DJ Leila Samir presents an hour every month.

NTS hosts since early doors, Loose Bones take you on a freewheeling trip through filmic soundscapes, psychedelia, vintage experimentalism, fuzzed out rock and other esoteric surprises. Every single month.

DJ, vinyl archivist and presenter Gary The Tall’s show’s sole objective is to shine a light to the environs of outsider artists and their innovative endeavours where he unveils obscure post-punk, dub, synth, psychedelic rock and left-field cosmic excursions. 
Follow him on Instagram here
Infamous Beirut DJ Ernesto Chahoud takes over the NTS signal for his monthly 'Beirut Daze' slot, recorded in Lebanon and showcasing music from his far-ranging collection.

Marsha by name and mellow by nature, MarshmeLLo takes you on a musical journey of all her influences plus a little extra. The first show ever broadcast on the station, Marsha continues to find her groove…
Australian duo Otologic traverse wave, post punk, outsider club & dubwise meanderings in constant delay.

nts1.n: DOWN AT THE END OF THE GARDEN W/ FLYING NUN RECORDS & Earth Tongue
nts1.d:
Aotearoa/New Zealand based Flying Nun Records present a monthly show of hidden gems and classics from the Nun catalogue, the nu-wave of the New Zealand underground and other curiosities from further afield. Join a revolving cast of Flying Nun alumni & friends for this monthly deep dive into the label's history and beyond.
From the host: "Vera and Lochie are joined by heavy psych-duo Earth Tongue at the Flying Nun record shop for a chat about their new album 'Dungeon Vision' and some of their favourite NZ selections."

nts1.j: CANADIAN POST PUNK, SYNTH & OTHER ODDITIES 1979-89

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ MUSIC 4 LOVERS
THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
Soup To Nuts w/ John Gómez

nts2.d:
A two hour journey through the utterly singular sound of Broadcast. Fronted by the late Trish Keenan, the Birmingham band melded the novel soundscaping of library music and early electronic experiments with their own sensitive take on indie rock and pop. Keenan's untimely passing in 2011 effectively ended the project - but a limited amount of previously unheard material has surfaced in the following years. 
This mix features classics and obscurities from across Broadcast's discography, including exclusive tracks featured in upcoming compilation "Distant Call - Collected Demos 2000-2006".
Contemplative, relaxing and emotional sounds from PC Music producer caro<3…
Japanese DJ Yuka Mizuhara joins us for an hour every month, selecting the most feel good classic house and disco. Dig!

nts2.n: ORGANIC MUSIC TOKYO W/ CHEE SHIMIZU (R)
nts2.d: The man behind Tokyo's revered Organic Music record store takes to the airwaves for a monthly 60 minute journey through his extensive collection…

nts2.n: FUSION BATCHES W/ DJ MORSE CODE & KING MOST (R)
nts2.d:
DJ / Producer Morse Code brings us a monthly all vinyl mix of jazz-funk, jazz fusion, and rare groove in the tradition of his Fusion Batches mixes from the mid 00's, which championed dollar bin heat as well as the rares, all in the name of the hunt for the best grooves!
From the host: "This episode features a special exclusive guest mix from King Most, a prolific editor, producer, and DJ hailing from The Bay Area and a long time friend. He blessed us with an incredible guest mix.  Enjoy!!"
Cuban record collector, ethnomusicologist researcher, picks up a hour of global vinyl via Havana every months.

nts2.n: NTS GUIDE TO: '70S ANGOLAN SEMBA & MÉRINGUE (R)
nts2.d: An hour of '70s Angolan Semba & Mergenue, two distinct styles which emerged during Angola’s liberation movement against Portuguese colonial rule through Angolan records labels such as Rebita & Merengue.

nts2.n: THE ULINE CATALOG W/ CALVIN LECOMPTE (R)
nts2.d: Leaf through songwriter Calvin LeCompte’s monthly selection via The Uline Catalog…

Tracing the links between the American primitive & finger picking guitar tradition with Indian Classical sounds, featuring American-style ragas from John Fahey, Robbie Basho & more, selected by Dan Breuer.

Selections from our European Primitive Guitar compilation, out now. 
EPG is a compilation of instrumental guitar compositions, mapping out the European analogues of the American Primitive Guitar movement, spearheaded by John Fahey in the 1950s.
Prolific London based DIY & lo-fi pop producer Keel Her heads to the studio for a show once a month.
Entering the light spirit body with KeiyaA, once a month.

nts2.n: PAVEL MILYAKOV: Feuerwerk W/ Timothy George Kelly, Dylan Spencer-Davidson

subtle.n: 5 Decades of Dub w/ Joe Vincent

subtle.d: ixxi and Fei take you on a percussive journey with their show syncop8, moving from downtempo ambient to breaks, polyrhythms, and high energy beats, exploring every rhythmic and textural nuance in between.
Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

Rolling through varied tempo's and genres, Jimmy 2 Shoes brings dance floor orientated vibes and flows to wriggle your toes to. Expect eclectic selections and the freshest sounds from the UK underground.

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
subtle.d: Tripta is a Toronto based producer/DJ focusing on the darker sounds of Grime. Also blending elements of Drill, Dubstep and even Jungle all together for a unique sound you won't hear anywhere else. Signed to Slimzee's 'Slimzo's Recordings' and ‘Rotpot Records’.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.n: Strictly Business Show w/ Qua Rush & Flipz MC
What's The Weather, Selecta? w/ Megan Wroe

subtle.d:
Hailing from Mumbai, now living in London, Chaitu is energised by the UK sound and scene.

With a love of all things weird, weighty and groovy; his mixes and productions bridge electro, techno, breaks, UKG and Jungle, with sprinklings of everything in between.

Chaitu champions provocative beats that break down the barriers of style, genre and expectation, all while keeping you moving.

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.n: Hannah Rubin and Noelle Armstrong – MELLOW DRAMA
Frankie Jax No Mad - Not Evil Radio
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
